title: saxifraga scardica subsp. scardica
intro: Saxifraga scardica subsp. scardica, also known as the Balkan saxifrage, is a perennial plant native to the Balkan Peninsula, characterized by its small, white or pinkish flowers and thick, fleshy roots. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which are attributed to its rich content of saponins and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Saxifraga scardica subsp. scardica have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion and kidney function, and even possessing antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies for various health issues, such as urinary tract infections and kidney stones.
